rationale: Both candidates independently rediscover the two GT threads visible in the diff (the trust_remote_code default flip True→False, and the deploy-config-override pipeline precedence fix), and both explicitly validate that the deploy-override bug was already resolved via the new test. X also surfaces a plausible additional gap (UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES silently skipped in the engine_client-missing branch) and engages with the endpoint_policy error-response pattern, giving it slightly broader coverage of the review surface. Y's extra findings (missing docstrings for a new kwarg, duplicate warning logs) are valid but lower-value nitpicks mislabeled as 'major', and it misses the mutable-PretrainedConfig caching concern GT flagged, same as X. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section. Precision and actionability are comparable across both — concrete file/line evidence and clear suggested fixes — with X's narrative review slightly better calibrated on severity.
